text:
Long ago, in a land where every day was like a fairytale, a peculiar old hag lived in a cozy cottage on the edge of a forest. Her name was Olive. Olive spent her days spinning yarns into intricate tapestries that told dreams. Despite her sparse appearance, she was known far and wide.

One autumn, a mysterious traveler stopped in Olive's hamlet. They had heard whispers of the old spinner's enchanting words. This visitor brought a gift—a handful of seeds, glowing with an otherworldly light. Olive knew these were special. They had once given life to a whole garden, a place where colors danced all year.

With trembling hands, Olive planted the seeds next to her spinning wheel. Day by day, they grew under her careful watch. They bloomed into a spectacular blossom: a starflower. Its glowing petals painted a myriad of colors on any surface they touched. One morning, as Olive leaned on her spindle, the starflowers danced around
